title: Resinous Polish Taco Stir-fry
description: A crumbly and flavorful twist on traditional Polish cuisine, featuring crunchy hard taco shells, savory stir-fry, and a hint of resinous notes.

ingredients.name: Ground pork
ingredients.notes: Make sure to cook to an internal temperature of 160°F (71°C)

ingredients.name: Onion
ingredients.notes: Finely chop the onion before adding to the stir-fry

ingredients.name: Bell pepper
ingredients.notes: Green, red, or yellow bell pepper works well in this dish

ingredients.name: Mushrooms
ingredients.notes: Button or cremini mushrooms add a nice earthy flavor

ingredients.name: Tomato
ingredients.notes: Cherry or grape tomatoes can be used for a sweeter flavor

ingredients.name: Resinous thyme
ingredients.notes: Use fresh thyme for the best flavor

ingredients.name: Garlic powder
ingredients.notes: Add a sprinkle of garlic powder for extra depth

ingredients.name: Olive oil
ingredients.notes: Use a neutral-tasting oil to avoid overpowering the dish

instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the ground pork and cook until browned, breaking it up with a spoon as it cooks.
Add the chopped onion and cook until translucent, then add the sliced bell pepper.
Add the mushrooms and cook until they release their moisture and start to brown.
Add the diced tomato and cook until the mixture is heated through.
Add the garlic powder, salt, and pepper to taste.
Add the crispy taco shells to the skillet and stir-fry for a few minutes to coat with the meat and vegetable mixture.
Add the fresh thyme leaves and stir-fry for another minute.
Let the dish stand for a few minutes before serving to allow the flavors to meld together.
